enhancements-026.quickRevisionNotes:
What: Performance optimization patterns for BAdIs that fire per item — eliminate SELECT inside loop.

Used for:
- Replacing per-item SELECT SINGLE with STATICS buffer that loads config on first call
- Using FOR ALL ENTRIES to prefetch all needed rows before the BAdI loop
- Choosing HASHED/SORTED tables with WITH KEY for O(1)/O(log n) in-memory lookup

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- STATICS: lt_config TYPE TABLE OF ztplant_config. IF lt_config IS INITIAL. SELECT INTO TABLE @lt_config. ENDIF.
- SELECT * FROM ztplant_config FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @lt_items WHERE werks = @lt_items-plant INTO TABLE @lt_config
- IF_EX_MB_MIGO_BADI~LINE_MODIFY hot path

Interview keyword: No SELECT inside loop — buffer or FOR ALL ENTRIES

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you identify a performance problem caused by an enhancement?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SAT — ABAP Runtime Analysis — use karke complete transaction profile karo. Yeh per method aur program consume hue time dikhata hai, tumhe identify karne dete hue ki enhancement code bottleneck hai kya. ST05 SQL Trace use karke sab database calls execution time aur call count ke saath dekho — 10,000 items process karne wali transaction mein 10,000 ka count dikhane wala SELECT clearly loop ke andar SELECT indicate karta hai. SM50 slow transaction ke dauraan active work processes aur running programs dikhata hai. Yeh teen tools together complete picture dete hain: time kahan spent hai, kya DB activity ho rahi hai, aur system work process blocked hai kya.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Use SAT — ABAP Runtime Analysis — to profile the complete transaction. It shows time consumed per method and program, letting you identify if enhancement code is the bottleneck. Use ST05 SQL Trace to see all database calls with execution time and call count — a SELECT showing count of 10,000 in a transaction that processes 10,000 items clearly indicates SELECT inside loop. SM50 shows active work processes and running programs during a slow transaction. These three tools together give a complete picture: where is the time spent, what DB activity is happening, and is the system work process blocked.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: The most important diagnostic discipline: measure before you change. Running SAT for 5 minutes gives you data to focus your optimization effort. Without measurement, you might spend hours optimizing the wrong thing.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Guessing which code is slow and optimizing the wrong thing. Always profile first. Also: running SAT in production during peak hours — run in DEV or QA with representative data volume for valid results.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SAT usage for enhancement performance:
" 1. Open SAT transaction
" 2. Set program filter to your BAdI class name
" 3. Click Record -> switch to transaction session
" 4. Execute the slow transaction with same data volume
" 5. Back to SAT -> Evaluate
" 6. Sort by 'Net Time' descending
" Key columns:
" Program = your ZCL_ class -> you own this code
" Net Time % = % of total execution time -> your share
" Calls = how many times this method was called
" If ZCL_BADI_IMPL->PROCESS shows 80% time = enhancement is bottleneck

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the FOR ALL ENTRIES clause and when should you use it in enhancements?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: FOR ALL ENTRIES IN single SELECT ko multiple key combinations ke liye ek database roundtrip mein data fetch karne deta hai, loop mein repeated SELECT SINGLE calls ki bajaye. Multiple document lines process karne wale enhancement code mein, pehle sab keys ko internal table mein collect karo, phir sab needed data load karne ke liye single FOR ALL ENTRIES SELECT karo, phir loop mein READ TABLE use karo — loop ke andar sirf memory operations. Critical precaution: FOR ALL ENTRIES se pehle hamesha check karo ki driving table empty nahi hai — agar empty hai, SAP WHERE condition ignore karta hai aur sab table rows return karta hai, full table scan cause karte hue.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: FOR ALL ENTRIES IN allows a single SELECT to fetch data for multiple key combinations in one database roundtrip, instead of repeated SELECT SINGLE calls in a loop. In enhancement code processing multiple document lines, collect all keys into an internal table first, then do a single FOR ALL ENTRIES SELECT to load all needed data, then use READ TABLE in the loop — only memory operations inside the loop. Critical precaution: always check that the driving table is not empty before FOR ALL ENTRIES — if it is empty, SAP ignores the WHERE condition and returns all table rows, causing a full table scan.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: FOR ALL ENTRIES is one of the top ABAP performance patterns. In an enhancement that processes 5,000 document lines, switching from SELECT SINGLE inside loop to FOR ALL ENTRIES can reduce DB calls from 5,000 to 1 — orders of magnitude faster. This is a fix worth knowing cold for interviews.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Not checking for empty driving table before FOR ALL ENTRIES. This is a classic bug — when no items exist, the SELECT returns entire table contents. Always wrap with IF lt_items IS NOT INITIAL.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" FOR ALL ENTRIES in enhancement — correct pattern
DATA: lt_items  TYPE TABLE OF mseg,
      lt_config TYPE TABLE OF ztplant_config.

" Step 1: Collect items
lt_items = get_all_items( ). " assume filled

" Step 2: FOR ALL ENTRIES SELECT — ONE DB call
IF lt_items IS NOT INITIAL.  " CRITICAL CHECK!
  SELECT werks, active, tax_code
    FROM ztplant_config
    FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @lt_items
    WHERE werks = @lt_items-werks
    INTO TABLE @lt_config.
ENDIF.

" Step 3: Use READ TABLE inside loop — memory only
LOOP AT lt_items INTO DATA(ls_item).
  READ TABLE lt_config INTO DATA(ls_cfg)
    WITH KEY werks = ls_item-werks.
  IF sy-subrc = 0.
    " Use ls_cfg — no DB call here
  ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.

followupAnswerBank.question: How does using STATICS help performance in BAdI implementations?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: STATICS ABAP mein method-local variables hain jo same session ke andar same method ke multiple calls mein apni value retain karte hain. Repeatedly called BAdI implementation mein — for example per document line ek baar — STATICS internal table use karo configuration data ko sirf first call pe IS INITIAL check ke saath load karne ke liye, phir sab subsequent calls memory mein STATICS table se padhte hain bina kisi database access ke. Yeh pattern N redundant SELECT statements eliminate karta hai jahan N processed document lines ki number hai. STATICS approach configuration ya master data ke liye most appropriate hai jo transaction ke dauraan change nahi hota.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: STATICS in ABAP are method-local variables that retain their value across multiple calls to the same method within the same session. In a BAdI implementation called repeatedly — for example once per document line — use a STATICS internal table to load configuration data only on the first call using an IS INITIAL check, then all subsequent calls read from the STATICS table in memory without any database access. This pattern eliminates N redundant SELECT statements where N is the number of document lines processed. The STATICS approach is most appropriate for configuration or master data that does not change during the transaction.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Real example: BAdI for goods receipt checking plant tax configuration. Plant config data is stable during a transaction. Using STATICS: first call loads from DB, next 999 calls use in-memory data. Measured improvement: transaction time dropped from 45 seconds to 4 seconds for 1000-line GRs.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Using STATICS for data that must be fresh every call — like posting date-dependent data or user-input-dependent data. STATICS are appropriate only for data that is stable for the duration of the calling transaction.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" STATICS pattern for config caching in BAdI
METHOD if_ex_mb_migo_badi~check_item.
  " STATICS persist across calls within same session
  STATICS: lt_plant_config TYPE TABLE OF ztplant_config,
           lv_loaded       TYPE xfeld.

  " Load only once — first call
  IF lv_loaded IS INITIAL.
    SELECT werks, active, taxcode
      FROM ztplant_config
      INTO TABLE @lt_plant_config.
    lv_loaded = 'X'. " Flag: do not load again
  ENDIF.

  " All calls use in-memory data — zero DB access after first
  READ TABLE lt_plant_config
    WITH KEY werks = ms_goitem-plant
    INTO DATA(ls_config).
  IF sy-subrc = 0 AND ls_config-active = 'X'.
    " Process item
  ENDIF.
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the impact of object creation inside a loop in enhancement code?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Loop ke andar CREATE OBJECT ya NEW operator use karke ABAP objects banana har loop iteration ke liye constructor overhead, memory allocation cost, aur garbage collection pressure increase karta hai. Jab enhancement thousands of document lines loop mein process karta hai, per iteration naya handler object banana yeh overhead multiply karta hai. Fix: loop se pehle object ko ek baar instantiate karo, phir loop ke andar different parameters ke saath uske methods repeatedly call karo — yeh ABAP pe applied flyweight pattern hai. Object state method parameters ke through reset hota hai, per iteration naya object banake nahi.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Creating ABAP objects inside a loop using CREATE OBJECT or NEW operator incurs constructor overhead, memory allocation cost, and increases garbage collection pressure for each loop iteration. When an enhancement processes thousands of document lines in a loop, creating a new handler object per iteration multiplies this overhead. Fix: instantiate the object once before the loop, then call its methods repeatedly with different parameters inside the loop — this is the flyweight pattern applied to ABAP. The object state is reset via method parameters, not by creating a new object per iteration.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: This is a subtle performance issue that code reviewers often catch. Developers who come from Java or Python backgrounds sometimes create objects inside loops habitually. In ABAP mass processing scenarios — batch jobs, IDocs, mass GR — this becomes measurable performance degradation.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Thinking object creation is free — in a 10,000 iteration loop, it adds up. Always review enhancement code for object creation inside loops when performance issues are reported. Also: not cleaning up large objects at the end of the enhancement method — SAP garbage collection handles it eventually but explicit FREE can help in memory-critical scenarios.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" BAD: object creation inside loop
LOOP AT lt_items INTO DATA(ls_item).
  DATA(lo_proc) = NEW zcl_item_processor( ). " <- NEW per item!
  lo_proc->process( ls_item ).
ENDLOOP.

" GOOD: object created once before loop
DATA(lo_proc) = NEW zcl_item_processor( ). " <- once only
LOOP AT lt_items INTO DATA(ls_item).
  lo_proc->process( ls_item ). " reuse same object
ENDLOOP.

" Even better: pass whole table to method
lo_proc->process_all( lt_items ). " one call, no loop

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle the scenario where a BAdI is called too frequently?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jab BAdI hook bahut frequently fire karta hai — for example screen mein har field change pe — performance degrade hoti hai kyunki har user interaction pe heavy processing chalti hai. Solutions: BAdI method ke start pe guard clause add karo jo check kare ki relevant triggering condition actually change hui ya nahi — last processed value track karne ke liye STATICS variable use karo aur nothing relevant change hone par early RETURN karo. BAdI hook choice reconsider karo — agar business logic allow kare toh on-change ki bajaye pre-save jaisa coarser granularity hook use karo. STATICS use karke computed results cache karo taki same computation same inputs ke liye session ke dauraan repeat na ho.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When a BAdI hook fires too frequently — for example on every field change in a screen — performance degrades because heavy processing runs on every user interaction. Solutions: add a guard clause at the start of the BAdI method that checks if the relevant triggering condition actually changed — use a STATICS variable to track the last processed value and RETURN early if nothing relevant changed. Reconsider the BAdI hook choice — use a coarser granularity hook like pre-save instead of on-change if business logic allows. Cache computed results using STATICS so the same computation is not repeated for the same inputs during a session.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Screen BAdIs in transactions like ME21N or VA01 can fire on every field navigation if not careful. A developer who does not account for this creates a sluggish UI that users immediately complain about — slow typing, delayed screen refresh. Guard clauses are the standard fix.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Implementing heavy logic in a field-level BAdI without guard clauses. Always think about how many times a BAdI fires per transaction versus per save. If it fires on every field change, your logic must be extremely lightweight or well-guarded.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Guard clause pattern for frequently-firing BAdI
METHOD if_ex_me_process_po_cust~process_item.
  " Track last processed key to avoid reprocessing
  STATICS: lv_last_banfn TYPE banfn,
           lv_last_bnfpo TYPE bnfpo.

  " Guard clause: return early if same item as last call
  IF me_po_item-po_item-banfn = lv_last_banfn AND
     me_po_item-po_item-bnfpo = lv_last_bnfpo.
    RETURN. " No change in relevant field -> skip
  ENDIF.

  " Update tracking variables
  lv_last_banfn = me_po_item-po_item-banfn.
  lv_last_bnfpo = me_po_item-po_item-bnfpo.

  " Do the actual processing only when needed
  perform_heavy_validation( me_po_item ).
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you optimize a SELECT statement inside a BAdI for better performance?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Enhancement code mein SELECT optimization: SELECT * ki bajaye sirf required fields select karo, data transfer volume reduce karte hue. Ensure karo ki WHERE clause indexed fields use kare — SE11 mein table ke liye Indexes tab check karo. Optimal access path ke liye WHERE mein primary key ya secondary index fields use karo. WHERE clause mein indexed fields pe functions avoid karo kyunki yeh index use prevent karte hain. Non-key fields ke saath one record fetch karte time SELECT SINGLE ki bajaye UP TO 1 ROWS use karo. Repetitive calls ke liye, STATICS use karke results buffer karo ya FOR ALL ENTRIES ke saath pre-fetch karo. ST05 SQL Trace use karke actual execution plan check karo verify karne ke liye ki index use ho raha hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SELECT optimization in enhancement code: select only required fields instead of SELECT *, reducing data transfer volume. Ensure WHERE clause uses indexed fields — check in SE11 Indexes tab for the table. Use primary key or secondary index fields in WHERE for optimal access path. Avoid functions on indexed fields in WHERE clause as they prevent index use. Use UP TO 1 ROWS instead of SELECT SINGLE when fetching one record with non-key fields. For repetitive calls, buffer results using STATICS or pre-fetch with FOR ALL ENTRIES. Check the actual execution plan using ST05 SQL Trace to verify index is being used.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: A single unoptimized SELECT in a BAdI that is called during mass processing is a multiplier problem. Optimize the SELECT once and the improvement is multiplied by the number of times the BAdI fires. This is why even a seemingly small SELECT optimization in enhancement code has large real-world impact.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: SELECT * when only 2 fields are needed — transfers unnecessary data over network. Not checking if WHERE clause field is indexed — can cause full table scan on a 50 million row table. And not testing with production-scale data volume in QA before go-live.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SELECT optimization examples in BAdI
" BAD: SELECT * with full table scan
SELECT * FROM ztax_master
  WHERE land1 = 'IN'   " non-indexed field -> full scan
  INTO TABLE @lt_tax.

" GOOD: Specific fields, indexed WHERE, limited result
SELECT taxcode, rate, valid_from
  FROM ztax_master
  WHERE land1 = @lv_country      " ensure this is indexed
    AND taxtyp = @lv_tax_type    " compound key field
    AND valid_from <= @sy-datum
  ORDER BY valid_from DESCENDING
  INTO TABLE @lt_tax
  UP TO 5 ROWS.  " limit result set
" Check SE11 -> ZTAX_MASTER -> Indexes tab -> land1 indexed?

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the ABAP memory bottleneck issue in enhancement and how to avoid it?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Enhancement code mein memory bottleneck tab hota hai jab per BAdI call large internal tables create hoti hain aur explicitly free nahi hoti — agar BAdI thousands of times call hota hai, memory usage accumulate hoti hai jab tak garbage collection catch up na kare. Yeh SM50 ya SM66 mein visible high extended memory usage cause karta hai. Fixes: large tables use karne ke baad immediately memory release karne ke liye FREE statement use karo. Large tables ki redundant copies banana avoid karo — jahan possible ho references ke saath kaam karo. Per call fresh internal tables banane ki bajaye calls ke across share ho sakne wale data ke liye STATICS use karo. Extended memory exhausted hone par SM50 work process list aur AL11 memory dumps ke liye memory usage monitor karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Memory bottleneck in enhancement code occurs when large internal tables are created per BAdI call and not explicitly freed — if the BAdI is called thousands of times, memory usage accumulates until garbage collection catches up. This causes high extended memory usage visible in SM50 or SM66. Fixes: use FREE statement after using large tables to release memory immediately. Avoid creating redundant copies of large tables — work with references where possible. Use STATICS for data that can be shared across calls instead of creating fresh internal tables per call. Monitor memory usage with SM50 work process list and AL11 for memory dumps if extended memory is exhausted.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Memory issues in enhancements are harder to diagnose than performance issues — they often appear as MEMORY_NO_MORE_PAGING dumps during mass processing. ST22 will show the dump, but tracing it to enhancement code requires looking at what was running and what large tables were allocated at that time.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Assuming ABAP garbage collection handles all memory management efficiently — in tight mass processing loops with large table allocations per iteration, explicit FREE is necessary. Also: reading large tables from DB into memory when only 2-3 records are needed — use targeted WHERE clause instead.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Memory management in BAdI method
METHOD if_ex_mb_migo_badi~check_item.
  DATA: lt_large_data TYPE TABLE OF ztransaction_log.

  " Load data needed for this call
  SELECT * FROM ztransaction_log
    WHERE mblnr = @iv_mblnr
    INTO TABLE @lt_large_data.

  " Process the data
  LOOP AT lt_large_data INTO DATA(ls_log).
    " ... process ...
  ENDLOOP.

  " Explicitly free large table after use
  FREE lt_large_data.  " <- Important for memory management
  " Without FREE: table stays in memory until method scope ends
  " With 10000 BAdI calls: 10000 x table size = memory leak
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you test enhancement code with production-like volumes in a non-production system?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Production-like volumes ke saath enhancement performance test karna: representative testing ke liye production se QA mein client copy ya data refresh request karo. Direct method call loop use karke UI bypass karte hue large synthetic dataset — for example 50,000 generated records — ke saath BAdI logic ko directly invoke karne ke liye dedicated test program banao. Volume ke under performance measure karne ke liye is test ke dauraan SAT profiler aur ST05 SQL Trace use karo. Results ko performance requirements ke saath compare karo — for example 1000 GR lines 60 seconds ke under complete hone chahiye. Headroom ensure karne ke liye minimum 150 percent expected peak volume pe test karo. Test results document karo aur go-live sign-off criteria mein include karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Testing enhancement performance with production-like volumes: request a client copy or data refresh from production to QA for representative testing. Create a dedicated test program that invokes the BAdI logic directly with a large synthetic dataset — for example 50,000 generated records — using a direct method call loop, bypassing the UI. Use SAT profiler and ST05 SQL Trace during this test to measure performance under volume. Compare results with performance requirements — for example 1000 GR lines must complete in under 60 seconds. Test at minimum 150% of expected peak volume to ensure headroom. Document test results and include in go-live sign-off criteria.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: A test program that directly calls the BAdI method in a loop with 100,000 records gives you a realistic performance baseline in 30 minutes in DEV — far faster than setting up production copy. This is a practical approach senior developers use to quickly validate enhancement performance before go-live.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Testing with 10 records in DEV and calling it done. Performance issues in enhancements are almost always volume-dependent — they simply do not appear with small datasets. Volume testing is non-negotiable for enhancements that run during mass processing.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Performance test program for BAdI logic
REPORT z_badi_perf_test.
DATA: lo_badi    TYPE REF TO if_ex_mb_migo_badi,
      lt_items   TYPE TABLE OF bapi_goodsmvt_item,
      lv_start   TYPE timestampl,
      lv_end     TYPE timestampl.
" Generate test data — 50000 items
DO 50000 TIMES.
  APPEND VALUE #( plant = 'TEST' matnr = |MAT{ sy-index }| )
    TO lt_items.
ENDDO.
" Record start time
GET TIME STAMP FIELD lv_start.
" Call BAdI logic directly
TRY.
    GET BADI lo_badi.
    LOOP AT lt_items INTO DATA(ls_item).
      CALL BADI lo_badi->check_item
        EXPORTING iv_plant = ls_item-plant.
    ENDLOOP.
  CATCH cx_badi_not_implemented.
ENDTRY.
" Record end time and report
GET TIME STAMP FIELD lv_end.
WRITE: / 'Time (sec):', ( lv_end - lv_start ).

enhancements-027.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: Enhancement khud ko koi special authorization nahi chahiye — yeh toh standard SAP transaction ke andar execute hota hai, aur standard transaction ki authorizations already user ke paas hain. Problem tab aati hai jab enhancement code andar se kuch extra kaam karta hai — jaise confidential data read karta hai (payroll amount, vendor price, customer credit limit) ya koi privileged action perform karta hai (posting, deletion, status change). Agar enhancement yeh kaam bina AUTHORITY-CHECK ke karta hai toh security hole ban jaata hai. Example: ek BAdI MIGO mein goods receipt process karta hai aur internally vendor purchase price bhi read karta hai — agar user ke paas vendor price dekhne ki authorization nahi hai but enhancement directly SELECT karta hai bina check ke — user effectively unauthorized data dekh sakta hai ya use kar sakta hai. Doosri problem: enhancement code mein AUTHORITY-CHECK hai lekin SY-SUBRC baad mein check nahi kiya — user authorization fail hota hai but code aage chalti rahti hai because developer ne SY-SUBRC = 0 check karna bhool gaya. Fix: har sensitive data access ya privileged operation ke baad AUTHORITY-CHECK daalo aur SY-SUBRC hamesha check karo. Yeh especially important hai payroll, FI (finance), SD pricing, aur vendor master enhancements ke liye.
enhancements-027.hinglishMasterExplanation:
Authorization in enhancements ek nuanced topic hai — interview mein depth dikhane ka mauka hai.

Concept 1 — Enhancement execution context:
Jab enhancement trigger hota hai, woh user ki existing session mein run hota hai. User ko pehle se transaction authorization mil chuki hoti hai — jaise MIGO ke liye M_MSEG_BWE check hua tha. Enhancement standard transaction ke upar additional logic add karta hai. Iska matlab enhancement automatic extra authority nahi deta — user ki same authorizations apply hoti hain.

Concept 2 — Aur authorization issue kab hoti hai?
Jab enhancement code kuch EXTRA karta hai jo standard transaction normally nahi karta. Example 1: BAdI MIGO mein internally payroll data read karta hai — HR_INFOTYPE_OPERATION ya direct SELECT FROM PA0008 — user ke paas HR read access nahi honi chahiye, lekin enhancement ne seedha read kiya. Example 2: BAdI sales order save pe competitor pricing table read karta hai for comparison — confidential Z-table — authorization object define nahi kiya. Example 3: Enhancement code user ki role check karte hua ABAP code mein hard-coded role names use karta hai — 'IF sy-uname = admin_user' — yeh authorization design bypass hai.

Concept 3 — Missing AUTHORITY-CHECK after read:
Enhancement mein code hai: AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'M_MSEG_BWE' ... Lekin NEXT LINE: SELECT FROM sensitive_table — developer ne authority-check rakha toh sahi, lekin SY-SUBRC check nahi kiya — agar check fail hua toh bhi code aage chal jaata hai. Yeh classic authorization bug hai — partial implementation.

Concept 4 — Authorization object design for enhancement:
Agar enhancement confidential data access karta hai, nayi authorization object define karo ya existing standard authorization object reuse karo. SE11 mein authorization object define karo — ZCUST_PRICE jaise. Users/roles mein yeh object assign karo. Enhancement mein AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'ZCUST_PRICE' likho. SY-SUBRC 0 hone pe hi sensitive data access karo. Yeh proper design hai.

Concept 5 — Audit trail:
Agar enhancement confidential data reads karta hai, access log maintain karna security best practice hai — SLG1 mein log karo ki kis user ne kab kaunsa sensitive data access kiya.

Concept 6 — Security review process:
Enhancement development mein security review mandatory step hona chahiye — especially agar enhancement sensitive business area mein hai (HR, FI, Payroll, Pricing). Security team se approval lena before transport to production.

Interview mein yeh framework present karo: Enhancement authorization = execution context + extra data access check + AUTHORITY-CHECK implementation + SY-SUBRC validation + access logging. Yeh comprehensive answer hai.
enhancements-027.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'Enhancements themselves do not require special authorization since they run within the user session that already has authorization for the standard transaction. However, when enhancement code accesses data or performs actions beyond what the standard transaction normally does — such as reading payroll amounts, vendor pricing, or performing privileged postings — it must include explicit AUTHORITY-CHECK statements for the relevant authorization objects. A common issue is adding an AUTHORITY-CHECK but failing to check SY-SUBRC afterward, which means the check runs but unauthorized users still proceed. For enhancements accessing confidential data, I define a custom authorization object in SE11, assign it to appropriate roles, and perform the authority check in the enhancement code before any sensitive data access. Access logging in SLG1 is also a good practice for audit trail purposes.'

Hinglish tip: 'AUTHORITY-CHECK ke baad SY-SUBRC check karna bhool jaana' — yeh specific gotcha mention karna interviewer ko impress karta hai. Ye dikhata hai ki tum sirf theory nahi jaante, real coding mistakes bhi jaante ho. Authorization object SE11 mein define karna bhi bonus point hai.
enhancements-027.codeExamples:
" Authorization issue example in BAdI
" BAD: Missing SY-SUBRC check after AUTHORITY-CHECK
METHOD if_ex_badi_vendor_price~read_price.
  " Authority check present but SY-SUBRC not validated!
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'M_LFB1_BUK'
    ID 'BUKRS' FIELD lv_bukrs
    ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'. " 03 = display
  " BUG: Missing: IF sy-subrc <> 0. MESSAGE ... RETURN. ENDIF.

  " This SELECT runs even if authority check failed!
  SELECT ekpo~netpr FROM ekpo
    WHERE lifnr = @lv_vendor INTO @lv_price.
ENDMETHOD.

" GOOD: Complete authorization implementation
METHOD if_ex_badi_vendor_price~read_price.
  " Custom authorization object for vendor pricing
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'ZVEND_PRICE'
    ID 'BUKRS' FIELD lv_bukrs
    ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.  " CRITICAL: Always check SY-SUBRC
    MESSAGE 'No authorization to view vendor pricing' TYPE 'E'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  " Only authorized users reach here
  SELECT SINGLE netpr FROM ekpo
    WHERE lifnr = @lv_vendor
    INTO @lv_price.
ENDMETHOD.
enhancements-027.interviewScriptEnglish: Enhancements themselves do not require special authorization since they run within the user session that already has authorization for the standard transaction. However, when enhancement code accesses data or performs actions beyond what the standard transaction normally does — such as reading payroll amounts, vendor pricing, or performing privileged postings — it must include explicit AUTHORITY-CHECK statements for the relevant authorization objects. A common issue is adding an AUTHORITY-CHECK but failing to check SY-SUBRC afterward, which means the check runs but unauthorized users still proceed. For enhancements accessing confidential data, I define a custom authorization object in SE11, assign it to appropriate roles, and perform the authority check in the enhancement code before any sensitive data access. Access logging in SLG1 is also a good practice for audit trail purposes.
enhancements-027.interviewScriptHinglish: enhancements khud koi special authorization nahi maangte kyunki yeh user session ke andar run karte hain jiske paas already standard transaction ki authorization hoti hai. Lekin jab enhancement code aise data ya actions access kare jo standard transaction normally nahi karta — jaise payroll amounts, vendor pricing, ya privileged postings — toh us code mein relevant authorization objects ke liye explicit AUTHORITY-CHECK statements honi chahiye. Ek common issue hai AUTHORITY-CHECK add karna lekin uske baad SY-SUBRC check karna bhool jaana, matlab check run toh hota hai lekin unauthorized users proceed kar jaate hain. Confidential data access karne wali enhancements ke liye main SE11 mein custom authorization object define karta hoon, appropriate roles mein assign karta hoon, aur sensitive data access se pehle enhancement code mein authority check karta hoon. SLG1 mein access logging bhi audit trail ke liye good practice hai.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Hard-coding user names or role names in enhancement code for access control — like checking SY-UNAME = 'ADMIN'.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: This is not maintainable and bypasses SAP authorization framework. Always use AUTHORITY-CHECK with proper authorization objects.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Not directing users to run SU53 when they report authorization errors.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Some developers try to read all AUTHORITY-CHECK code manually to find the issue. SU53 shows you instantly. Also: SU53 only shows the LAST failed check — if user had multiple authorization issues, run the transaction step by step and check SU53 after each failure.

commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: FIELD DUMMY as a quick fix to suppress authorization errors in enhancement code — this completely bypasses the check and grants access to everyone. Never use FIELD DUMMY except in very specific justified cases with security team approval.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Assuming that if the standard transaction has authorization checks, the enhancement's additional writes are also covered — they are not.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Enhancement code's own write operations need their own authorization checks. Standard transaction checks cover standard program flow only.

wordByWordSamjho.meaning: Statement that checks user authorization against an auth object — must be followed by SY-SUBRC check.
Standard SAP auth object for vendor company code access — controls who can view vendor data.
Custom authorization object created for the vendor pricing scenario — checked with ID BUKRS and ACTVT.
Authorization field binding — BUKRS field of the auth object is checked against current company code.
Activity code 03 = display — typical for read-only price viewing scenarios.
Carries auth check result — 0 means authorized; non-zero means denied and code must return/error.
Error message used when SY-SUBRC <> 0 to fail the operation with explicit no-authorization text.
Statement that exits the method early on auth failure so no further processing leaks unauthorized data.
Reads vendor net price from purchase document item EKPO — only executed after successful auth check.

enhancements-027.quickRevisionNotes:
What: Authorization handling inside BAdI implementations — adding extra checks for sensitive data access.

Used for:
- Adding custom authorization objects (ZVEND_PRICE) when BAdI accesses sensitive fields
- Always checking SY-SUBRC after AUTHORITY-CHECK to prevent unauthorized SELECT execution
- Following auth check failure with MESSAGE TYPE 'E' and RETURN

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'ZVEND_PRICE' ID 'BUKRS' FIELD lv_bukrs ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'. IF sy-subrc <> 0. MESSAGE ... TYPE 'E'. RETURN. ENDIF.
- SU21 for auth object creation; PFCG for role assignment; SU53 for failed auth analysis
- Tables: EKPO (vendor net price), LFB1 (vendor company code)

Interview keyword: AUTHORITY-CHECK then mandatory SY-SUBRC then RETURN

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you define a custom authorization object for an enhancement?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Custom authorization object define karne ke liye: SE11 open karo, new Authorization Object banao — Z se shuru hone wala naam, for example ZVENDOR_PRICE — ise relevant authorization object class jaise SD ya MM ke saath assign karo, aur authorization fields add karo jaise company code ke liye BUKRS aur activity type values 01 create, 02 change, 03 display ke liye ACTVT. Object activate karo. Ise PFCG mein appropriate roles mein permitted field values ke saath assign karo. Enhancement code mein, AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'ZVENDOR_PRICE' ID 'BUKRS' FIELD lv_bukrs ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03' use karo aur uske baad hamesha turant SY-SUBRC check karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: To define a custom authorization object: open SE11, create a new Authorization Object — name starting with Z, for example ZVENDOR_PRICE — assign it to a relevant authorization object class such as SD or MM, and add authorization fields like BUKRS for company code and ACTVT for activity type values 01 create, 02 change, 03 display. Activate the object. Assign it to appropriate roles in PFCG with permitted field values. In the enhancement code, use AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'ZVENDOR_PRICE' ID 'BUKRS' FIELD lv_bukrs ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03' and always check SY-SUBRC immediately after.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Custom authorization object is the professional solution when enhancement accesses data beyond standard transaction scope. It gives security team full control over who sees what — without changing enhancement code. Adding new user access = just update the role in PFCG, no code change needed.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Hard-coding user names or role names in enhancement code for access control — like checking SY-UNAME = 'ADMIN'. This is not maintainable and bypasses SAP authorization framework. Always use AUTHORITY-CHECK with proper authorization objects.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SE11: Create Authorization Object ZVENDOR_PRICE
" Object Class: SD
" Fields:
"   BUKRS (type BUKRS) - Company Code
"   ACTVT (type ACTVT_AUTH) - Activity: 03=Display, 06=Delete

" PFCG: Assign to role Z_MM_BUYER
"   ZVENDOR_PRICE: BUKRS = 1000, ACTVT = 03

" Enhancement code:
AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'ZVENDOR_PRICE'
  ID 'BUKRS' FIELD lv_company_code
  ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.  " 03 = Display
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  MESSAGE e001(zauth) WITH 'Vendor pricing view not authorized'.
  RETURN.
ENDIF.
" Safe to access price data now

followupAnswerBank.question: What is SU53 and how do you use it to debug authorization failures in enhancements?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SU53 current user ke liye ya specified user ke liye last failed authorization check display karta hai. Jab enhancement AUTHORITY-CHECK trigger karta hai jo fail ho, user ko error message dikhta hai. Tum ya user phir SU53 chala sakte ho exactly dekhne ke liye ki kaunsa authorization object fail hua, kaunse field values check hue, user's role kya values provide karta hai, aur kya required tha. Yeh information PFCG mein user's role update karne ke liye use hota hai missing authorization add karne ke liye. Enhancement debugging mein, SU53 use karne ke liye first tool hai jab users authorization errors report karte hain — yeh exactly woh object aur field combination dikhata hai jise grant karna chahiye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SU53 displays the last failed authorization check for the current user or for a specified user. When an enhancement triggers an AUTHORITY-CHECK that fails, the user sees an error message. You or the user can then run SU53 to see exactly which authorization object failed, which field values were checked, what values the user's role provides, and what was required. This information is used to update the user's role in PFCG to add the missing authorization. In enhancement debugging, SU53 is the first tool to use when users report authorization errors — it shows the exact object and field combination that needs to be granted.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: SU53 saves hours of authorization debugging. Without it, you would have to read through the AUTHORITY-CHECK code, trace which object and fields are checked, then compare with user role assignments. SU53 shows the exact failure in one screen. Learn SU53 — it comes up in every authorization discussion.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Not directing users to run SU53 when they report authorization errors. Some developers try to read all AUTHORITY-CHECK code manually to find the issue. SU53 shows you instantly. Also: SU53 only shows the LAST failed check — if user had multiple authorization issues, run the transaction step by step and check SU53 after each failure.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SU53 usage for enhancement authorization debug:
" 1. User runs MIGO, gets error: 'No authorization'
" 2. User runs SU53 (or you run SU53 -> Enter Username)
" 3. SU53 shows:
"    Object: ZVENDOR_PRICE
"    Field BUKRS: Required = 1000, User has = (blank)
"    Field ACTVT: Required = 03,   User has = (blank)
" 4. Go to PFCG -> find user's role -> add ZVENDOR_PRICE
"    with BUKRS = 1000, ACTVT = 03 -> regenerate -> transport
" 5. User retests -> authorized now

" In enhancement code — ensure AUTHORITY-CHECK is present:
AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'ZVENDOR_PRICE'
  ID 'BUKRS' FIELD lv_bukrs
  ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  MESSAGE 'Missing authorization' TYPE 'E'. " <- SU53 captures this
ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: Can an enhancement accidentally grant more authorizations than intended?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Haan, enhancements inadvertently privilege escalation grant kar sakti hain. Examples: aisi enhancement jo trusted RFC destination ya service user context use karke function module call karti hai woh higher privileges ke saath execute ho sakti hai jo calling user ke paas nahi hain. Aisi enhancement jo ABAP authority bypass statements use karke ya SUBMIT ya CALL TRANSACTION ke through privileged programs call karke data read karti hai effectively sab enhancement users ko privileged context jaisa same access deti hai. Prevention: enhancement code mein kabhi authority bypass use mat karo, hamesha calling user's context ke saath AUTHORITY-CHECK perform karo, sab users ke liye accessible enhancements se privileged RFC destinations call karna avoid karo. RFC calls ya cross-system access involve karne wali enhancements ko security architect se review karwao.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Yes, enhancements can inadvertently grant privilege escalation. Examples: an enhancement that calls a function module using a trusted RFC destination or a service user context may execute with higher privileges than the calling user has. An enhancement that reads data using ABAP authority bypass statements or calling privileged programs via SUBMIT or CALL TRANSACTION effectively gives all enhancement users the same access as the privileged context. Prevention: never use authority bypass in enhancement code, always perform AUTHORITY-CHECK with the calling user's context, avoid calling privileged RFC destinations from enhancements accessible to all users. Have a security architect review enhancements that involve RFC calls or cross-system access.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Privilege escalation through enhancements is a real security finding in SAP security audits. Auditors look for AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT '...' FIELD DUMMY bypass patterns, SELECT with sy-uname override, and RFC calls in enhancement code. Knowing these patterns shows security awareness.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Using AUTHORITY-CHECK ... FIELD DUMMY as a quick fix to suppress authorization errors in enhancement code — this completely bypasses the check and grants access to everyone. Never use FIELD DUMMY except in very specific justified cases with security team approval.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" DANGEROUS pattern — authority bypass in enhancement
AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'M_LFB1_BUK'
  ID 'BUKRS' FIELD 'DUMMY'.  " <- compares against literal 'DUMMY' — always fails; real bypass is `ID 'BUKRS' DUMMY` without quotes
" The DUMMY keyword (no quotes) skips the field check entirely, granting all users access

" SAFE pattern — proper check with actual field value
AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'M_LFB1_BUK'
  ID 'BUKRS' FIELD lv_bukrs  " actual company code checked
  ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  MESSAGE 'No authorization for company code' TYPE 'E'.
  RETURN. " <- hard stop, no bypass
ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: What authorization considerations apply to enhancements that post data to the database?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Database mein data post karne wali enhancements ko write operation perform karne se pehle create ke liye ACTVT 01 ya change ke liye 02 check karna chahiye. Agar enhancement custom Z-table mein insert karti hai, us table ke liye BUKRS aur ACTVT fields wale authorization object define karo aur INSERT se pehle check karo. Posting function modules ya BAPIs call karne wali enhancements ke liye, called FM apni authorization checks perform karta hai — but enhancement privileged context ke saath call karke unhe bypass nahi karni chahiye. CALL FUNCTION IN UPDATE TASK use karne wali enhancements same user session context mein chalti hain, isliye user authorizations apply hote hain. Enhancement code ke sab write operations ko security review ke dauraan document karo taki authorization concept unhe cover kare.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Enhancements that write or post data must check ACTVT 01 for create or ACTVT 02 for change before performing the write operation. If the enhancement inserts into a custom Z-table, define an authorization object for that table with BUKRS and ACTVT fields and check it before INSERT. For enhancements that call posting function modules or BAPIs, the called FM performs its own authorization checks — but the enhancement must not bypass those by calling with privileged context. Enhancements using CALL FUNCTION IN UPDATE TASK run in the same user session context, so user authorizations apply. Document all write operations in enhancement code during security review so the authorization concept covers them.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Security audit finding: enhancement writes to a custom table without authorization check — any user who can run the transaction can write to that table regardless of their intended access level. This is a critical finding. Always add authorization check before any INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE in enhancement code.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Assuming that if the standard transaction has authorization checks, the enhancement's additional writes are also covered — they are not. Enhancement code's own write operations need their own authorization checks. Standard transaction checks cover standard program flow only.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Authorization check before write in enhancement
METHOD if_ex_mb_migo_badi~post_document.
  " Check authorization before INSERT to custom audit table
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'ZAUDIT_LOG'
    ID 'BUKRS' FIELD lv_bukrs
    ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '01'.  " 01 = Create
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    " Log the failed attempt but do not stop GR posting
    " (audit logging failure is not a business process block)
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  " Authorized: proceed with INSERT
  INSERT INTO zaudit_gr_log VALUES @ls_audit_record.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    MESSAGE 'Audit log insert failed' TYPE 'W'.
  ENDIF.
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle authorization in a filter-dependent BAdI with multiple implementations?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Filter-dependent BAdI implementations apne specific filter context ke liye chalti hain — for example company code 1000 implementation A trigger karta hai aur company code 2000 implementation B trigger karta hai. Har implementation ko apne data access scope se relevant authorization checks independently perform karne chahiye. BAdI filter mechanism execution route karta hai but authorization grant nahi karta — company code 1000 filter context ke assigned user ab bhi 2000 data access kar sakta hai agar implementation A bina checking cross-company data read karta hai. Har implementation ko apne specific company code aur data scope ke liye authority independently check karni chahiye. Yeh assume mat karo ki filter assignment authorization imply karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Filter-dependent BAdI implementations each run for their specific filter context — for example company code 1000 triggers implementation A and company code 2000 triggers implementation B. Each implementation must independently perform authorization checks relevant to its data access scope. The BAdI filter mechanism routes execution but does not grant authorization — a user assigned to company code 1000 filter context could still access 2000 data if implementation A reads cross-company data without checking. Each implementation should check authority for its specific company code and data scope independently. Do not assume filter assignment implies authorization.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: This is an advanced authorization question that shows you understand the distinction between routing (what filter does) and authorization (what AUTHORITY-CHECK does). Interviewers asking about filter BAdIs and authorization want to see this distinction explicitly stated.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Thinking BAdI filter = authorization boundary. It is not — filter controls which implementation runs, but the implementation must still perform its own authorization checks for the data it accesses.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Each filter-dependent implementation checks its own auth
" Implementation A: for BUKRS = 1000
METHOD if_ex_zfin_badi~process.
  " Do NOT assume filter = authorized
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
    ID 'BUKRS' FIELD '1000'  " explicit check for 1000
    ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    RETURN. " Not authorized even though filter matched
  ENDIF.
  " Access company 1000 specific financial data
ENDMETHOD.

" Implementation B: for BUKRS = 2000 — same pattern
METHOD if_ex_zfin_badi~process.
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
    ID 'BUKRS' FIELD '2000'  " separate explicit check
    ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0. RETURN. ENDIF.
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the risk of using SUBMIT or CALL TRANSACTION inside an enhancement?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Enhancement ke andar SUBMIT ya CALL TRANSACTION use karna significant security risk hai kyunki yeh enhancement user ko aise programs ya transactions trigger karne deta hai jin ke liye woh directly authorized nahi ho sakte. CALL TRANSACTION normally transaction authorization checks perform karta hai, but additional parameters wala SUBMIT interactive authorization screens bypass kar sakta hai. Agar sab warehouse users ke liye accessible enhancement payroll ya HR program ke liye SUBMIT call karti hai, woh users effectively payroll processing access gain karte hain jo unhe nahi hona chahiye. Security rule: explicit security review aur justification ke bina enhancements mein SUBMIT ya CALL TRANSACTION use mat karo. Iski bajaye explicit AUTHORITY-CHECK wale BAPI calls ya function modules use karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Using SUBMIT or CALL TRANSACTION inside an enhancement is a significant security risk because it allows the enhancement user to trigger programs or transactions they may not be directly authorized for. CALL TRANSACTION normally performs transaction authorization checks, but SUBMIT with additional parameters can bypass interactive authorization screens. If an enhancement accessible to all warehouse users calls SUBMIT to a payroll or HR program, those users effectively gain access to payroll processing they should not have. Security rule: do not use SUBMIT or CALL TRANSACTION in enhancements without explicit security review and justification. Use BAPI calls or function modules with explicit AUTHORITY-CHECK instead.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: SAP security auditors specifically look for SUBMIT and CALL TRANSACTION statements in enhancement code — they are flagged in automated security scans. If found, you must justify why they are needed and prove authorization is not bypassed. Avoiding them entirely is simpler and safer.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Using SUBMIT ... AND RETURN in enhancement code for convenience without security review. This is a shortcut that creates audit findings. Use proper BAPI or FM calls with explicit authorization checks instead.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" RISKY: SUBMIT inside enhancement
" This could give warehouse user payroll program access!
METHOD if_ex_mb_migo_badi~post_document.
  SUBMIT rproc000  " payroll program!
    WITH... AND RETURN.  " <- bypass risk!
ENDMETHOD.

" SAFER: Use BAPI with proper authorization
METHOD if_ex_mb_migo_badi~post_document.
  " Explicit authorization check first
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'ZPAYROLL_ACC'
    ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '01'.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0. RETURN. ENDIF.
  " Use BAPI — it performs its own checks internally
  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE'
    EXPORTING... " proper parameter passing
  " BAPI does standard auth checks — safer than SUBMIT
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you document authorization requirements for an enhancement during development?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Enhancement ke liye authorization documentation cover karna chahiye: enhancement name aur triggered transaction, accessed data ki complete list — read aur written tables — sensitivity classification ke saath, enhancement code mein checked sab standard authorization objects, koi bhi custom authorization objects unke field specifications ke saath defined, woh roles jinhe new authorization objects aur unke permitted values include karne ke liye update karna chahiye, aur production transport se pehle security team review sign-off. Yeh documentation Basis security team ko role updates mein support karta hai aur go-live security checklist ka part banta hai. Regulated industries ke liye, yeh document compliance audits ke liye mandatory hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Authorization documentation for an enhancement should cover: the enhancement name and triggered transaction, complete list of data accessed — tables read and written — with sensitivity classification, all standard authorization objects checked in the enhancement code, any custom authorization objects defined with their field specifications, roles that must be updated to include new authorization objects and their permitted values, and a security team review sign-off before production transport. This documentation supports the Basis security team in role updates and forms part of the go-live security checklist. For regulated industries, this document is mandatory for compliance audits.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Security team cannot update roles correctly without knowing what authorization objects the enhancement uses. Missing authorization documentation = missing role updates = users get 'no authorization' errors on go-live day = P1 ticket. Documentation prevents this entirely.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Skipping authorization documentation and only providing functional specification. Functional spec tells what the enhancement does — security documentation tells what authorization checks exist and what role changes are needed. Both are required for a complete handover.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Authorization documentation template (as code comment)
"============================================
" ENHANCEMENT: ZCL_MIGO_VENDOR_PRICE_BADI
" TRANSACTION: MIGO
" TRIGGERED BY: MB_MIGO_BADI->POST_DOCUMENT
"--------------------------------------------
" DATA ACCESS:
"   READ:  EKPO (purchase order prices) - CONFIDENTIAL
"   READ:  ZVEND_PRICE_LOG (custom audit log)
"   WRITE: ZVEND_AUDIT (custom write)
"--------------------------------------------
" AUTHORIZATION CHECKS:

followupAnswerBank.question: What happens if you forget to transport a new authorization object to production?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Agar custom authorization object production mein transport nahi hua but enhancement code AUTHORITY-CHECK mein use reference karta hai, behavior SAP configuration pe depend karta hai: kuch setups mein, missing authorization object check ko bina restriction pass karne deta hai — security hole create karte hue jahan koi user check nahi hota. Doosron mein yeh runtime error cause kar sakta hai. Safe transport sequence yeh hai: pehle authorization object definition transport karo, phir enhancement implementation, phir new object include karne wali role changes. Missing object transport common go-live issue hai — ise transport checklist mein explicitly include karo aur go-live se pehle SUIM ya SE11 mein verify karo ki target system mein object exist karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: If a custom authorization object is not transported to production but the enhancement code references it in AUTHORITY-CHECK, the behavior depends on SAP configuration: in some setups, a missing authorization object causes the check to pass without restriction — creating a security hole where no user is checked. In others it may cause a runtime error. The safe transport sequence is: first transport the authorization object definition, then the enhancement implementation, then the role changes that include the new object. Missing object transport is a common go-live issue — include it explicitly in the transport checklist and verify in SUIM or SE11 that the object exists in the target system before go-live.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: This is a real go-live mistake pattern: developer transports enhancement but forgets the custom authorization object. In production, AUTHORITY-CHECK on a non-existent object may silently pass — all users get access. Security audit later finds the gap. Proper transport checklist prevents this.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Assuming that all objects in the transport request are automatically in the right order. Authorization objects must be explicitly added to transport requests and transported before the code that references them. Check SE11 in target system to verify object existence after transport import.
